creator: dirk.meyer@dinoex.sub.org
text:
> >Release:        4.2-RELEASE
>
> Unfortunately, although the ports entry lists no requirements, in
> fact it does require a libcrypto.so.2 to run. 

Dependency is recorded when package is build under 4.2 or 4.1

--- snip ----
.if ${OSVERSION} < 430000
[...]
LIB_DEPENDS+=   crypto.2:${PORTSDIR}/security/openssl

> up a comment that this library comes from OpenSSL; this is listed in
> the ports collection but has no package (at least for 4.2; for 5.0
> a package link is provided, but it is broken -- no such package exists!)
> Tracked the sources link to fetch the original openssl distribution
> (openssl-0.9.6b) and built it with the "shared" option.  But it only
> produces a libcrypto.so.0.9.6 -- not .so.2.

Please use the version in the ports ...

/usr/ports/security/openssl/Makefile

diff openssl/Makefile openssl/Makefile
--- openssl/Makefile	Sun Jul 22 05:24:01 2001
+++ openssl/Makefile	Mon Sep  3 20:40:37 2001
@@ -16,7 +16,7 @@
 MAINTAINER=	ports@FreeBSD.org
 
 .include <bsd.port.pre.mk>
-.if exists(/usr/lib/libssl.a) && ${OSVERSION} >= 400014
+.if exists(/usr/lib/libssl.a) && ${OSVERSION} >= 430000
 FORBIDDEN=	"OpenSSL is already in the base system"
 .endif
 
or just comment out the line:
FORBIDDEN=      "OpenSSL is already in the base system"

then "make install" or "make package".

repeat this in /usr/ports/security/openssh:

then "make install" or "make package".

after this the installation of OpennSSH works.

kind regards Dirk

- Dirk Meyer, Im Grund 4, 34317 Habichtswald, Germany
